title: 70 St. Mary Axe
notes:
CWSTs Clean and Disinfect

To attend site and carry out a clean and disinfect of the CAT 5 Break Tank - Level 21 LSS Room, and Break Tank - Basement 01 Bin Store/Loading Bay

***Chemical to sit for 30 minutes before clean due to the build up of scale.***

To drain, clean and disinfect cold water storage tank(s) only

Works will include post cleaning contact spray disinfection of the tank internals using BPR registered Silver Hydrogen Peroxide for 1 hour. Tank to be refilled with fresh water, checked for residual Hydrogen Peroxide, and returned to service CAT 5 BREAK TANK LEVEL 21 AND CAT 5 BREAK TANK BASEMENT TO BE CLEANED.  

1 x WTP  Showerhead Plus 5L  WTP Showerhead Plus (5litre)  Shower cleaner & sanitiser

title: Berkeley Square House
notes:
CONTRACT SOFTENED WATER TANK AND COOLING TOWER CLEANING SCHEDULE and DOEMSTIC SYSTEM DISINFECTION 

6 MONTHLY - in hours - 4 days
APRIL (pack out) 3 men
Please take borescope pictures inside packing of all packing 

OCTOBER (pack in)  in hours - 4 days
Please take borescope pictures inside packing of all packing 


2 days -

Saturday 

AM - 2 Engineers + security/ site engineer. Hydrocert to disinfect 11th floor tanks with Sanosil at 35ppm. Hydrocert ( 2 engineers) plus site engineer to pull though all outlets. 
PM -  4 Engineers to start cleaning 2 towers 

Sunday  - 3 Engineers 
2 x CT clean and disinfect entire system. 


**- October tower clean/disinfection (pack-in) is now to take place during normal working hours and WILL NOT include cleaning and disinfection of the basement and roof softened water tanks.
- April tower clean/disinfection remains unchanged i.e. OOH, pack-out, includes basement and roof softened water tanks.**

title: Rubicon
notes:
Notes from previous visit: CWST Clean and Spray Disinfect

Rubicon

To attend site and carry out a clean and spray disinfect of the CWST 1 and 2. 

This is a confined space tank clean. The internal conditions of these tanks were found to be contaminated with scale on the walls and sediment at the bottom of the tank following our recent PPM visit in April as per visit report V183700.
